name: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
description: Connect Microsoft Defender for Endpoint to monitor device health, security alerts, and vulnerability management across your organization.

config.setupInstructions:
1. Go to Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → App registrations
2. Click 'New registration', name it (e.g. 'CompAI Defender'), register
3. Note the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID
4. Go to 'Certificates & secrets' → 'New client secret', copy the value
5. Go to 'API permissions' → 'Add a permission' → 'APIs my organization uses'
6. Search for 'Microsoft Threat Protection' (or 'WindowsDefenderATP')
7. Select 'Application permissions' and add: Machine.Read.All, Alert.Read.All, Vulnerability.Read.All
8. Click 'Grant admin consent' for your organization
9. Paste the Tenant ID, Client ID, and Client Secret below

credentialFields.label: Tenant ID
credentialFields.helpText: Your Azure AD (Entra ID) tenant ID. Found in Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Overview.

credentialFields.label: Client ID (Application ID)
credentialFields.helpText: The Application (client) ID from your Azure AD app registration.

credentialFields.label: Client Secret
credentialFields.helpText: A client secret generated for your Azure AD app registration.

checks.name: Device List
checks.description: Retrieves devices managed by Microsoft Defender for Endpoint and verifies they are active and reporting.

checks.name: Monitoring & Alerting
checks.description: Checks for unresolved security alerts in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint and reports severity-based findings.

checks.name: Vulnerability Scanning
checks.description: Retrieves vulnerabilities detected by Microsoft Defender for Endpoint and reports critical and high severity findings.
